title: ClickHouse INSERT Throughput and Batch Row Efficiency
spl:
index=database sourcetype="clickhouse:query_log"
| where query_kind="Insert"
| eval rows_written=tonumber(written_rows)
| eval dur_ms=tonumber(query_duration_ms)
| eval rows_per_sec=if(dur_ms>0, round(1000*rows_written/dur_ms,0), null())
| bin _time span=15m
| stats median(rows_per_sec) as med_rps p10(rows_written) as p10_batch_rows by host, _time
| where p10_batch_rows < 1000 AND med_rps < 50000
description: Tiny insert batches inflate part count and merge pressure even when nominal QPS looks fine. Monitoring median throughput and batch row percentiles exposes anti-patterns from application drivers before merge backlogs form.
value: Guards ingestion pipelines against accidental per-row inserts that degrade cluster-wide performance.
implementation: Ensure `written_rows` and `query_kind` are extracted for HTTP and native insert paths. Baseline med_rps per workload; tune thresholds per table. Pair with `clickhouse:parts` active_parts panel. Work with app teams to raise batch sizes or use async insert settings.
visualization: Line chart (med_rps, p10_batch_rows), Scatter (written_rows vs duration).
knownFalsePositives: Planned full or differential backups, log backup bursts, and restore drills can spike I/O and job duration; suppress or threshold against the known backup window.
grandmaExplanation: We follow Oracle instance and space signals so the team can keep service levels and archivelog management in line with how the business actually uses the database.
